paper_title: High Resolution Sequence Stratigraphic Analysis of the Late Miocene Abu Madi Formation, Northern Nile Delta Basin

methods.description: This method involves subdividing seismic 3rd order depositional sequences into sub-seismic scale cycles to aid in hydrocarbon reservoir description. It uses the concept of parasequences and parasequence sets to analyze the stratigraphic architecture.

steps.description: Obtain lithologic logs and gamma ray responses from ten wells in the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch lithologic logs and gamma ray data from wells

steps.description: Arrange the wells in four cross sections running from south to north to distinguish changes in pattern and sequence number.
steps.searchable_summary: arrange wells into cross sections for analysis

steps.description: Perform high resolution sequence stratigraphic analysis using the lithologic logs and gamma ray responses.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stratigraphic sequences using logs and gamma ray data

steps.description: Classify parasequences into progradational, retrogradational, or aggradational parasequence sets based on shoreline movement and grain size trends.
steps.searchable_summary: classify parasequences into sets based on stratigraphic data
